  2. ロール定義ページで「XXユーザ」を選択します。
  3. 「ユーザへのロールの割り当て」ダイアログで、ロールの割り当て先として使用可能なユーザおよびチームを選択し、「OK」を選択します。
SAML 属性を使用してロールをマッピング

SAP Analytics Cloud では、SAML ロールマッピングを使用すると、ユーザの SAML 属性に基づいてロールをユーザに自動的に割り当てることができます。詳細は第５章カスタムIdPを利用した、ユーザ・権限管理をご参照ください。

カスタムIdPを利用した、ユーザ・権限管理

SAML２認証を利用することで、SAP Analytics Cloudへのシングルサインオン（SSO）機能が安全に実現され、ユーザは複数のログイン情報を覚えることなくSAP Analytics Cloudにアクセスできます。

SAML２概要

SAML 2 フェデレーション認証には、2つのコンポーネントが関わっています。
アイデンティティプロバイダー（IdP）: ユーザを認証し、成功した場合にはサービスプロバイダー（SP）へ認証情報を提供します。SAP Analytics Cloudは標準でSAP Cloud IdentityをIdPとして使用しますが、お客様自身で準備したSAML 2に基づいたIdPを設定することも可能です。
サービスプロバイダー（SP）: IdPによるユーザ認証を基にサービスを提供します。SAP Analytics Cloudやそのバックエンドデータソース（HANA、BW、S4/HANA、など）は、同一のIdPを通じてユーザ認証を行います。

以下がそのプロセスフローの一例です：

  1. ユーザがChromeブラウザを使ってSAP Analytics Cloudにログインしようとします。
  2. SAP Analytics CloudはSAMLリクエストを作成し、これに応答します。
  3. ブラウザがユーザをアイデンティティプロバイダーのページにリダイレクトします。
  4. アイデンティティプロバイダーはSAMLリクエストを解析し、ユーザが既に認証されているかどうかを確認します。
  5. ユーザがまだ認証されていなければ、アイデンティティプロバイダーは認証を求めます。すでに認証されている場合は、このステップを省略して、IdPは直接SAMLレスポンスを生成します。
  6. IdPはエンコードされたSAMLレスポンスをブラウザ経由で返します。
  7. ブラウザはそのSAMLレスポンスをSAP Analytics Cloudに送信し、検証を行います。
  8. 検証が成功すれば、ユーザはSAP Analytics Cloudにログインされ、様々なリソースへのアクセス権を得ます。

カスタム SAML アイデンティティプロバイダの有効化

デフォルトでは、SAP Cloud Identity サービスは SAP Analytics Cloud によって使用されます。カスタム SAML アイデンティティプロバイダを利用するには、カスタムIdPを有効化する必要があります。
設定の詳細は下記のURLをご参照ください。
カスタム SAML アイデンティティプロバイダの有効化 - SAP Help Portal

終わり

今回はユーザ管理/権限管理 機能のご紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは予算計画などの一連の予算管理業務に必要な機能を備えており、本ブログシリーズではそれぞれの機能の特徴や要点をご紹介しています。こちらのリンクからご参照ください。

entry.title: Introducing fileUploadXLS: The Ultimate Excel Custom Widget for SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
fileUploadXLS custom widget is designed to streamline the process of handling Excel or XLS files within SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), enabling users to easily retrieve file data in various formats such as JSON, raw text, and more.

Key Features

  • File Format Flexibility: Supports Excel and XLS files, ensuring compatibility with common data sources.
  • Data Retrieval: Easily retrieve file data in JSON, raw text, or other formats.
  • Filtering Capabilities: Apply filters to records based on value or character presence.
  • Data Hierarchy: Set hierarchical structures for better data organization.

Installation Guide

Watch Full Guidance on YouTube As well

To integrate the fileUploadXLS widget into your SAP application, follow these simple steps:

  1. Download the Widget:

    Click the link above to download the fileUploadXLS.json file.

    Or Download it from Github : https://github.com/SAP-Custom-Widget/fileUploadXLS

  2. Add to SAC Portal:

    • Navigate to your SAC Portal.
    • Select "Analytic Application" from the left sidebar.
    • Go to the "Custom Widget" tab.
    • Click the "+" icon on the right side and upload the fileUploadXLS.json file you downloaded.

  3. Start Using the Widget:

    • Once uploaded, the widget is ready to use in your applications.

Methods Overview

The widget offers a range of methods to interact with your data:

Set Methods

MethodParameter TypeDescription
setHierarchyStructure(hierarchy)Selection/Object arraySets the hierarchical structure for the data.
setFilterByValue(filterByValue)Selection/Object arrayApplies a filter to records based on a constant value.
setFilterByContains(filterByContains)Selection/Object arrayApplies a filter to records based on a value that contains a specific character.

Get Methods

MethodReturn TypeDescription
getDataAsJson()Selection/Object arrayRetrieves uploaded data in JSON/Selection/Object format.
getDataAsRaw()stringRetrieves uploaded data in raw string format.
getTotalRecordsCount()integerGets the total count of records.
getHeaders()string[] arrayGets the headers of uploaded data as an array.

I hope you find the SAC-P Custom Widget: fileUploadXLS useful for your projects. Your feedback and suggestions are always welcome, as they help in making this tool even better. Thank you for your support, and happy data handling!

Conclusion

The SAC-P Custom Widget: fileUploadXLS is a powerful addition to SAP Analytics Cloud, designed to simplify the process of working with Excel and XLS files. With its robust data retrieval capabilities and flexible filtering options, it enhances your ability to manage and manipulate data efficiently. By following the easy installation steps, you can quickly integrate this widget into your applications and start leveraging its features immediately.

entry.title: Important Notice: Changes in SAP Analytics Cloud Classic Stories & Analytic Applications
entry.content:
Introduction

The Classic Experience for Stories and Analytic Applications has been the original user interface of our product, which has been serving our customers and partners for many years. However, as technology evolves and user expectations change, we have decided to deprecate the Classic Experience and other non-strategic features and replace it with the Optimized Experience, a platform that combines Stories, Analytic Applications, and core Digital Boardroom features using the existing licensing structure. It offers customers more features, versatility, and better performance and usability with one consistent experience.

This blog post will explain the reasons for the deprecation, the benefits of the Optimized Experience, and the key timelines for the transition. We hope that this blog post will help you prepare for the change and make the most of the Optimized Experience.

 

Why Deprecate the Classic Experience?

The Classic Experience has been a reliable and stable user interface for our product, but it also has some limitations and drawbacks that affect the user experience and performance. These are some of the main reasons for the deprecation are:

  • Performance & Usability: the feedback we received from customers was that the Classic Experience had some challenges when it came to performance and usability that would impact the adoption of the application.
  • Unification: there was confusion on which asset (Stories, Analytic Applications, or the Digital Boardroom) to use based on the business use case. It meant that the development effort was high and would lead to a different experience depending on the asset that was used.
  • Innovation: we have been focused on delivering innovation on the Optimized Experience. It means that customers are not benefiting from the new features and improvements that we are making based on customer feedback via Customer Roundtables or the Influence Portal.

 

What are the Benefits of the Optimized Experience?

The Optimized Experience is a platform that combines Stories, Analytic Applications, and core Digital Boardroom features using the existing licensing structure. It offers customers more features, versatility, and better performance and usability with one consistent experience.

For more details on the benefits, please read the external blog posts under the Resource section.

 

Timeline and Implications

We have started to phase out the Classic Experience and other features that are not part of our strategy. These include:

  • Classic Stories (includes Grid Pages and Smart Discovery)
  • Analytic Applications (integrated with Stories) (Blog)
  • Classic Digital Boardroom (integrated with Stories) (Blog)
  • Low Adoption Features in Classic Stories and Analytic Applications (Documentation)
  • Search to Insight (which is replaced with Just Ask for Cloud Foundry Tenants)
  • Allocations (which is replaced with Data Action Allocation Step)
  • Point of Interests 

We aim to implement gradual stages that will help customers throughout the process. There are a few that have already been made available within the product. For example,

  • Within the Allocations Module there is a warning to guide users on the New Data Action Allocation Step

 

  • With the 2024 Q2 QRC release of SAP Analytics Cloud that is made available to all customers on Saturday, May 18, 2024, we have introduced an in-app Deprecation Warning for Classic Stories and Analytic Applications

There are a few more timelines to be aware of to understand the implications for the transition. DISCLAIMER this is the current state of planning and may be changed by SAP at any time:

  • As of the 2024 Q4 QRC: new tenants that are created will NOT have access to the non-strategic features that were mentioned above. It means that Content Packages that have been created will fail to import assets that are unsupported.
  • As of Early 2025 H1: existing customers will be prevented from creating new or duplicating unsupported assets. For these assets, customers will still be able to view, edit, and share. There will be a new configuration option for System Admins to setup up an auto deletion of assets that have not been viewed beyond a certain period.
  • As of Late 2025 H2: customers will no longer be able to access unsupported assets. You will need to migrate your content before the deadline.

 

How to Prepare

We understand that this transition may require some planning and preparation on your part. Here are some steps you can take to prepare for the transition when it comes to Classic Stories and Analytic Applications:

  • Learn more about the Optimized Story Experience and its benefits by reading the external blog posts under the Resource section.
  • Learn more about the features that will not be made available in the Optimized Experience. It is available via our in-app documentation HERE.
  • Start migrating your existing Classic Stories and Analytic Applications to the Optimized Story Experience by using the “Convert to Optimized Design Experience” option in under File – Save in the toolbar.

 

Conclusion

The deprecation of the Classic Experience and the transition to the optimized experience is a strategic decision that will benefit our customers and partners in the long run. We appreciate your understanding and cooperation in this process, and we are committed to providing you with the best user experience and performance possible.

 

Resources

Highlight of New Features Exclusive to the Optimized Experience

  • Composites
  • Replace a Model
  • Presentation Mode

Benefits of Integration of Analytic Applications

  • New Design Experience
  • Unification of Stories and Analytic Applications

Performance and Usability Improvements

  • Active Viewport Rendering
  • Lite Viewer
  • Key Improvements and Features
  • Performance and Usability Improvements

entry.title: Create Custom Tables in SAP Analytics Cloud with dynamoTable
entry.content:
dynamoTable custom widget is designed to help you create dynamic, customizable tables within SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), enhancing your data presentation and analysis capabilities.

Key Features

  • Custom Table Creation: Easily create and manage tables to suit your specific data needs.
  • Flexible Data Handling: Input data as an array of objects for seamless integration.
  • Simple Integration: Quickly add the widget to your SAC applications with straightforward installation steps.

Installation Guide

YouTube Video Tutorial

To integrate the dynamoTable widget into your SAP application, follow these simple steps:

  1. Download the Widget:

    Click the link above to download the dynamoTable.json file.

    Or find on GitHub: https://github.com/SAP-Custom-Widget/dynamoTable 

  2. Add to SAC Portal:

    • Navigate to your SAC Portal.
    • Select "Analytic Application" from the left sidebar.
    • Go to the "Custom Widget" tab.
    • Click the "+" icon on the right side and upload the dynamoTable.json file you downloaded.

  3. Start Using the Widget:

    • Once uploaded, the widget is ready to use in your applications.

Methods Overview

The widget offers a simple method to manage your table data:

MethodReturn TypeDescription
setTableData(arrayOfObject)Selection/Object arraySets the table data using an array of objects.

Example Usage

Here’s an example of how to use the setTableData method to populate your table:

 

var data  = [
  {
    productId: "1",
    productName: "Wireless Mouse",
    category: "Electronics",
    price: "25.99",
    quantitySold: "120",
    dateOfSale: "2024-05-01"
  },
  {
    productId: "2",
    productName: "Bluetooth Headphones",
    category: "Electronics",
    price: "79.99",
    quantitySold: "75",
    dateOfSale: "2024-05-02"
  }
];

dynamoTable_1.setTableData(data);

 

Output

Conclusion

The SAC-P Custom Widget: dynamoTable is a powerful tool for creating and customizing tables in SAP Analytics Cloud. By following the simple installation steps, you can enhance your data presentations and streamline your analytics processes. Explore the features of dynamoTable and take your data handling to the next level.

entry.title: Boosting Capabilities: Extensibility for SAP Companion in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud is an all-in-one cloud platform that integrates business intelligence, planning, and predictive analytics. It provides real-time insights, business-driven data discovery, and seamless collaboration capabilities, allowing organizations to make informed, data-driven decisions. With SAP Analytics Cloud, businesses can unite their data in one secure and fully managed environment, enabling users to create dynamic, interactive dashboards, perform powerful data exploration, and run ad-hoc reporting with ease.

Partnered with tools like SAP Companion, a core module of the SAP Enable Now solution, SAP continues to provide comprehensive training, continuous learning, and in-app support to end-users. Further embracing this tradition of innovation, SAP Companion is now extending its expertise to SAP Analytics Cloud, the Data & Analytics cloud service offering from SAP.

On top of the standard embedded content provided by SAP for a while now (read more here), SAP Companion now comes with extensibility options. This means customers can create their own custom enablement content on top of what SAP provides out of the box!

SAP Companion for SAP Analytics Cloud

Available for release this year, the SAP Companion for SAP Analytics Cloud ensures you have a helping hand who provides you quick tips and guidance, always keeping you up-to-date with changes. It eliminates the need to navigate away from the application to access help content or user assistance, thereby significantly improving user experience. Key enhancements include:

Contextual Hotspot Help Content: Now, users can use "hotspots" to easily navigate their way around the user interface. When a hotspot is selected, it provides a summary explanation along with full help topics on the SAP Help Portal for a more detailed understanding.

Embedded Video Tutorials: Instead of just relying on help text, users can now watch embedded video tutorials related to their active screen. These videos can be viewed in a popover without leaving the application, enhancing the learning experience.

Interactive Tutorials: The new update features custom interactive tutorials that allow for self-directed learning. Users can directly engage with screen-related scenarios in a recorded system environment, boosting their familiarity with the SAP Analytics Cloud tools.

Quick Overview of Updates with What's New: SAP Companion for SAP Analytics Cloud provides concise summaries of updates about new or changed features in recent releases, ensuring you are always informed about the latest enhancements.

Direct Links to Full Documentation: For a deeper dive into the specific features, direct links to relevant full help sections on the SAP Help Portal are available.

How can you benefit?

You can benefit from these improvements directly by setting up your own custom content for SAP Analytics Cloud. Follow the instructions in the SAP Companion Integration Guide to set up your custom in-app help for SAP Analytics Cloud (remember that an SAP Enable Now license is needed).

Here are the steps you need to follow:

  1. Create an SAP Companion configuration for SAP Analytics Cloud in your Manager based on the script template already available
  2. Set your settings a necessary, taking care to check specifically for tracking, translation, and custom content settings
  3. Copy the configuration URL out of the Manager using the link symbol on the very right:

  1. Link your SAP Analytics Cloud instance to your SAP Enable Now configuration by logging in to SAP Analytics Cloud and then navigating to System > Administration > Custom Companion URL and pasting in the configuration URL.

The SAP Companion Integration Guide covers steps 1-3. There’s no need to reach out to SAP Analytics Cloud support for #4, simply add the URL yourself or ask your admin to do it for you if you don’t have access to the administration settings.

SAP Analytics Cloud and Continuous Learning

These updates to SAP Analytics Cloud provide powerful learning tools for all users, regardless of whether they are beginners or experienced. The proficiency levels achieved using the updated SAP Companion significantly simplify the discovery of relevant help information.

With SAP Companion for SAP Analytics Cloud, users get basic information via contextual popovers while links to full SAP Help Portal documentation ensure a deeper understanding of technical requirements. In the upcoming Q2 2024 release all these features are planned to be available to SAP Analytics Cloud users operating on a quarterly release cycle.

In conclusion, users of SAP solutions can benefit hugely from SAP Companion’s extensibility, including its application to SAP Analytics Cloud. Improved user experience and heightened adoption amplify the benefits associated with deployed applications. If you’re interested in learning how to improve your SAP systems' user experience, consider connecting with your SAP representative today.

entry.title: SAP Data Analyst Certification
entry.content:
Certificates won’t get you a job in data analytics.

But you know what might?

The real world skills you gain in the process of getting that certification.

I fell into the trap of certification collecting earlier on in my data career, thinking the more the merrier!

But when it came time for interviews, no one ever asked me about any specific certifications I had on my resume.

Instead, they asked about the tools I learned, projects I built, and how I tied my learnings to my everyday job.

One certification that teaches you data analyst skills in the context of real world tools and business use cases is SAP’s Data Analyst Certification.

Its courses teach you everything you need to work in SAP Analytics Cloud–one of the most popular platforms for analytics and planning.

The best part: if you’re a woman, veteran, career changer, or in a minority group, you’re eligible to take this cert completely free.

Start learning today at 👉 https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-data-analyst-sap-analytics-cloud?cam...
